question: JD-9/JD-11/JD-5 + RV-9 carry-forward: wire bench judge, thread a deterministic comparison_id, feed EscalationConfig, and (D-P4-1) record the judge's A/B->arm map so the kappa join is frame-correct?
migration: bench judge verb reads arm names+model ids+judge.escalation from the LOCKED experiment.yaml (canaries derived from the spec). Every judge_verdict carries a deterministic comparison_id (was Optional None) making Phase-3's RV-9 gate reliable end-to-end. judge_verdict gains an ADDITIVE arm_map field (A/B->physical arm), a slice of AN-1 pulled forward per D-P4-1 so the judge-vs-human kappa join resolves both winners to one arm frame. Guarded field addition on a hash-chained event: old verdicts lack it (analyze falls back to the assumed frame), no chain invalidated, genesis/verdict-schema tests checked. EscalationConfig threaded into kappa_by_class (was hardcoded 0.6/20); orders:single flagged; CANT_JUDGE excluded from kappa and duplicate verdicts deduped (JD-5). Implementation: Phase 4 slices 4A/4C.
author: jyang

question: JD-12/REVIEW-D-4 + JD-13: migrate the hash-chained verdict confidence field from float to the low|medium|high enum, and extend packet_sha256 to cover the render framing?
migration: Verdict.confidence changes TYPE (float -> low|medium|high enum) on a hash-chained judge_verdict event [D-4]. Compatibility: a field_validator(mode=before) coerces a legacy float to its band (confidence_bucket: <0.4 low, <0.75 medium, else high), so old-shape verdicts still read; new verdicts carry the enum. The client stops discarding the parsed value (was hardcoded 0.5/0.8) and derives the band from the judge's parsed confidence (conservative min across the two orders), or low when the orders disagreed. confidence is NOT part of packet_sha256, so no packet-hash impact. Greenfield (no production ledgers) so the compatibility surface is a documented note, not a live migration. Separately [JD-13], packet_sha256 now folds in a framing fingerprint (system prompt + body scaffolding + fence scheme, via a placeholder fence and sentinel content so it is content/order-independent), so a framing change is provenance-detectable; the fenced packet body [JD-8] keeps agent-authored content in the data channel. Implementation: Phase 5 slices 5I (confidence) and 5G.2 (packet).
author: jyang

question: JD-13 remainder: honor the spec's 'response labels assigned randomly per call' (seeded per-call shuffle), or amend the spec to the deterministic both-orders scheme?
rationale: Confirmed by jyang at Phase-6 start. Amend eval2.spec.md:184-185: response labels are assigned by a fixed both-orders scheme (AB then BA, judge/client.py _pos_to_arm) which cancels position bias BY CONSTRUCTION because both orders always run (config.orders=='both'); this supersedes 'assigned randomly per call'. Per-call randomization would add a nondeterministic surface (against CLAUDE.md determinism-by-default) for no bias-reduction benefit. Phase 5 already extended packet_sha256 to the render framing (the JD-13 provenance half). No client behavior change; a test encodes the accepted scheme so a future silent switch to a single order or a mislabeled position is caught. Implementation: Phase 6 slice 6F.
author: jyang

question: RN-18 fake-provider exhaustion (judge/providers/fake.py:109): silently replay the last scripted response, or raise?
rationale: Confirmed by jyang at Phase-6 start. FakeProvider raises a clear error when its scripted responses are exhausted (self._i >= len(self._responses)) instead of replaying the last item via min(self._i, len-1), per CLAUDE.md fail-loudly: the silent replay can hide a miscounted test script. Raising will surface any test that over-relies on the replay (audit test_eval2_plan.py, test_eval2_client.py, test_eval9_process.py); those are latent bugs, tightened explicitly with sign-off, not grandfathered. Implementation: Phase 6 slice 6D.
author: jyang

rationale: Phase-7 D-1 truth-up: the judge is IDENTITY-blind (never sees arm identities), not outcome-blind — the blind packet includes per-response holdout results by design (judge/packet.py), so judge_preference is deliberately NOT independent of holdout_pass_rate. This is disclosed as a [computed]-tagged provenance line in every analyze render and the term 'outcome-blind' is corrected to 'identity-blind' in the master plan and README. No behavior change; a naming/disclosure truth-up so judge agreement is never mistaken for an independent signal.
author: jyang
